source: CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
title: Minimize cluster access to read-only for Container Image repositories

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
input_row.title: Minimize cluster access to read-only for Container Image repositories
input_row.description: Note: GCR is now deprecated, see the references for more details. Configure the Cluster Service Account with Artifact Registry Viewer Role to only allow read-only access to AR repositories. Configure the Cluster Service Account with Storage Object Viewer Role to only allow read-only access to GCR.
input_row.rationale: The Cluster Service Account does not require administrative access to GCR or AR, only requiring pull access to containers to deploy onto GKE. Restricting permissions follows the principles of least privilege and prevents credentials from being abused beyond the required role. Impact: A separate dedicated service account may be required for use by build servers and other robot users pushing or managing container images. Any account granted the Storage Object Viewer role at the project level can view all objects stored in GCS for the project.
input_row.audit: For Images Hosted in AR: Using Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Artifacts Browser by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/artifacts 2. From the list of repositories, for each repository with Format Docker 3. Under the Permissions tab, review the role for GKE Service account and ensure that only the Artifact Registry Viewer role is set. Using Command Line: gcloud artifacts repositories get-iam-policy <repository-name> --location <repository-location> The output of the command will return roles associated with the AR repository. If listed, ensure the GKE Service account is set to "role": "roles/artifactregistry.reader". For Images Hosted in GCR: Using Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Storage Browser by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/storage/browser 2. From the list of storage buckets, select artifacts.<project_id>.appspot.com for the GCR bucket 3. Under the Permissions tab, review the role for GKE Service account and ensure that only the Storage Object Viewer role is set. Using Command Line GCR bucket permissions gsutil iam get gs://artifacts.<project_id>.appspot.com The output of the command will return roles associated with the GCR bucket. If listed, ensure the GKE Service account is set to "role": "roles/storage.objectViewer". If the GKE Service Account has project level permissions that are inherited within the bucket, ensure that these are not privileged: gcloud projects get-iam-policy <project_id> \ --flatten="bindings[].members" \ --format='table(bindings.members,bindings.role)' \ --filter="bindings.role:roles/storage.admin OR bindings.role:roles/storage.objectAdmin OR bindings.role:roles/storage.objectCreator OR bindings.role:roles/storage.legacyBucketOwner OR bindings.role:roles/storage.legacyBucketWriter OR bindings.role:roles/storage.legacyObjectOwner" Your GKE Service Account should not be output when this command is run.
input_row.remediation: For Images Hosted in AR: Using Google Cloud Console: 1. Go to Artifacts Browser by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/artifacts 2. From the list of repositories, for each repository with Format Docker 3. Under the Permissions tab, modify the permissions for GKE Service account and ensure that only the Artifact Registry Viewer role is set. Using Command Line: Add artifactregistry.reader role gcloud artifacts repositories add-iam-policy-binding <repository> \ --location=<repository-location> \ --member='serviceAccount:<email-address>' \ --role='roles/artifactregistry.reader' Remove any roles other than artifactregistry.reader gcloud artifacts repositories remove-iam-policy-binding <repository> \ --location <repository-location> \ --member='serviceAccount:<email-address>' \ --role='<role-name>' For Images Hosted in GCR: Using Google Cloud Console: For an account explicitly granted access to the bucket: 1. Go to Storage Browser by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/storage/browser. 2. From the list of storage buckets, select artifacts.<project_id>.appspot.com for the GCR bucket. 3. Under the Permissions tab, modify permissions of the identified GKE Service Account via the drop-down role menu and change to the Role to Storage Object Viewer for read-only access. For an account that inherits access to the bucket through Project level permissions: 1. Go to IAM console by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/iam-admin. 2. From the list of accounts, identify the required service account and select the corresponding pencil icon. 3. Remove the Storage Admin / Storage Object Admin / Storage Object Creator roles. 4. Add the Storage Object Viewer role - note with caution that this permits the account to view all objects stored in GCS for the project. 5. Click SAVE. Using Command Line: For an account explicitly granted to the bucket: Firstly add read access to the Kubernetes Service Account: gsutil iam ch <type>:<email_address>:objectViewer gs://artifacts.<project_id>.appspot.com where: • <type> can be one of the following: o user, if the <email_address> is a Google account. o serviceAccount, if <email_address> specifies a Service account. o <email_address> can be one of the following: ▪ a Google account (for example, someone@example.com). ▪ a Cloud IAM service account. Then remove the excessively privileged role (Storage Admin / Storage Object Admin / Storage Object Creator) using: gsutil iam ch -d <type>:<email_address>:<role> gs://artifacts.<project_id>.appspot.com For an account that inherits access to the GCR Bucket through Project level permissions, modify the Projects IAM policy file accordingly, then upload it using: gcloud projects set-iam-policy <project_id> <policy_file> Default Value: The default permissions for the cluster Service account is dependent on the initial configuration and IAM policy. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/container-registry/docs/ 2. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/how-to/service-accounts 3. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/how-to/iam
input_row.impact: A separate dedicated service account may be required for use by build servers and other robot users pushing or managing container images. Any account granted the Storage Object Viewer role at the project level can view all objects stored in GCS for the project.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- GCP APIs can query both Artifact Registry and Google Cloud Storage IAM policies.
- IAM role assignment is a standard GCP service property related to access control.
- The audit field provides `gcloud` and `gsutil` commands to verify IAM roles for both AR and GCS.
- This control does not require visual inspection or SSH access, rendering it suitable for automation.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_iam_artifact_registry_read_only_access_enforced

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Google Cloud SDK `gcloud artifacts repositories get-iam-policy` command to retrieve the IAM policy for each Artifact Registry repository.
- Use the Google Storage CLI `gsutil iam get` to retrieve the IAM policy for the designated GCR bucket.
- Enumerate GKE Service Accounts with access to each AR repository and GCR bucket.
- Validate that the GKE Service Account has the `roles/artifactregistry.reader` role for AR repositories.
- Validate that the GKE Service Account has the `roles/storage.objectViewer` role for the corresponding GCR bucket.
- Ensure that the GKE Service Account does not have any more privileged roles such as `roles/storage.admin` or `roles/storage.objectAdmin`.
- A repository fails the check if a GKE Service Account is found with more privileged roles.
- Pass Criteria: All GKE Service Accounts associated with AR or GCR have only read-only roles specified.
- Fail Criteria: Any GKE Service Account associated with AR or GCR has roles beyond those intended for read-only access.
step2_review:
---

### 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: **AGREE**

- **Manual vs Automated:** The classification as "Automated" is correct. Both Artifact Registry and GCS (for legacy GCR buckets) expose IAM policies via GCP APIs and are fully queryable using CLI tools (`gcloud`, `gsutil`).
- **GCP SDK Capabilities:** 
  - `gcloud artifacts repositories get-iam-policy` retrieves IAM policies for Artifact Registry repositories.
  - `gsutil iam get` retrieves IAM policies for GCS buckets (which back GCR).
  - Service Account enumeration and role validation can be fully automated.
- **CloudFormation/GCP Config:** GCP Config (now "Config Controller") can also be used, but is not required for this check. CloudFormation is AWS-specific and not relevant here.

---

### 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: **Score: 8/10**

- **Correctness of Automation Details:**
  - **Artifact Registry:** `gcloud artifacts repositories get-iam-policy` is correct.
  - **GCR (GCS):** `gsutil iam get` is correct for GCS buckets.
  - **Role Validation:** Checking for `roles/artifactregistry.reader` and `roles/storage.objectViewer` is appropriate.
  - **Privilege Escalation:** Correctly identifies that more privileged roles (e.g., `roles/storage.admin`) should be flagged.
- **Missing/Incomplete Aspects:**
  - **Service Account Identification:** The process for mapping GKE clusters to their node service accounts is not described. This is non-trivial, as GKE clusters may use node service accounts, workload identity, or other mechanisms. The automation should explicitly describe how GKE service accounts are discovered and mapped to AR/GCR access.
  - **Role Inheritance:** The check should account for roles granted at higher levels (project, org) that may flow down to AR/GCR resources.
  - **Program Name:** `gcp_iam_artifact_registry_read_only_access_enforced` is descriptive, but omits GCR and is not explicit about "read-only" being the enforced policy. A more precise name would help.

---

### 3. IMPROVEMENTS

- **Service Account Discovery:** 
  - Explicitly document how GKE service accounts are discovered. For node pools, this is the node service account; for Workload Identity, this may be workload-specific.
  - Consider using `gcloud container clusters describe` and `gcloud iam service-accounts list` to enumerate relevant service accounts.
- **Role Inheritance:**
  - Ensure the audit checks for roles assigned at the project or org level that may grant excessive permissions to GKE service accounts.
  - Use `gcloud projects get-iam-policy` and `gcloud organizations get-iam-policy` as needed.
- **Granularity:**
  - Consider whether the check should validate *only* the minimum required roles are present (i.e., no extra roles at any level).
- **Program Name:** 
  - Suggest: `gcp_gke_image_registry_read_only_access_enforced`
  - Or, if splitting AR and GCR: 
    - `gcp_gke_artifact_registry_read_only_access`
    - `gcp_gke_gcr_read_only_access`
- **Alternative API Methods:**
  - For completeness, mention that the REST API (`projects.locations.repositories.getIamPolicy` for AR, `buckets.getIamPolicy` for GCS) can also be used for automation.
- **Documentation:** 
  - Add a note that GCR is deprecated and recommend focusing on Artifact Registry for new clusters.

---

### 4. CONFIDENCE: **HIGH**

- The approach is fundamentally sound and leverages standard GCP CLI/API methods.
- Minor gaps in service account discovery and role inheritance handling, but these are addressable.

---

### 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: **Modify**

- **Modify** the automation details to:
  - Explicitly describe the process for discovering GKE service accounts (node and workload identity).
  - Ensure the audit checks for inherited IAM roles at the project and org levels.
  - Update the program name to reflect both AR and GCR, and clarify the "read-only" enforcement.
- The control remains suitable for automation, but the implementation details should be tightened for completeness and clarity.

---

**Summary Table:**

| Section             | Assessment         |
|---------------------|-------------------|
| Approach Validation | AGREE             |
| Technical Accuracy  | 8/10              |
| Improvements        | See above         |
| Confidence          | HIGH              |
| Recommendation      | MODIFY            |

---

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that all required information (IAM roles for Artifact Registry and GCR/Cloud Storage buckets) is fully accessible via GCP APIs and CLI tools (`gcloud`, `gsutil`). The audit steps map directly to API calls, and there is no need for manual inspection or console-only verification. The only nuance is ensuring the correct mapping of GKE service accounts, but this is feasible with GCP APIs.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
gcp_iam_gke_service_account_read_only_image_repo_access

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP Resource/Service:**  
  - Artifact Registry repositories (Format: Docker)  
  - Google Cloud Storage buckets (for legacy GCR)
  - GKE cluster node service accounts

- **API/CLI Method:**  
  - `gcloud artifacts repositories list` (to enumerate AR repos)  
  - `gcloud artifacts repositories get-iam-policy` (to get AR IAM policies)  
  - `gsutil ls -p <project>` (to list GCS buckets for GCR)  
  - `gsutil iam get gs://<bucket>` (to get GCS IAM policies)  
  - `gcloud container clusters describe` (to identify GKE node service accounts)

- **Properties to Check:**  
  - For each AR repository and GCR bucket, enumerate all IAM bindings for GKE node service accounts.
  - Confirm that GKE service accounts have only `roles/artifactregistry.reader` (AR) or `roles/storage.objectViewer` (GCR).
  - Ensure no more privileged roles (e.g., `roles/artifactregistry.admin`, `roles/storage.admin`, `roles/storage.objectAdmin`) are assigned to these service accounts.

- **Pass Criteria:**  
  - All GKE node service accounts have only the minimum required read-only roles (`roles/artifactregistry.reader` for AR, `roles/storage.objectViewer` for GCR) and no higher privileges.

- **Fail Criteria:**  
  - Any GKE node service account has additional or more privileged roles on AR or GCR resources.

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
Both AI opinions agreed on automation. The only minor gap was the explicit mapping of GKE service accounts, which is feasible via GCP APIs and included in the automation details above.

**SOURCE:** CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
